name: Batman Noir
description: B&W high-contrast comic-noir — ink-and-shadow, deep blacks, hard rim light from one side, weathered surfaces, no plastic gloss.
use_for: dramatic narrative covers, vigilante/crime scenes, brooding character portraits, anything that wants pure ink-and-paper craft.

dominant.role: deep black shadows occupying 60% of frame

flux.positive_prefix: Batman-noir monochrome ink-and-mass illustration, 60% black shadow holding the frame, single hard rim light from one side, rough paper texture, ink-mass shadows with no smooth digital shading, single saturated red accent permitted on ONE focal element (bat-signal / blood / ribbon), Mike Mignola Hellboy register.
flux.positive_suffix: Monochrome black ink on cream paper with one optional red accent, single dramatic figure, deep ink-mass shadows, jagged angular silhouette edges.

always_add: 60% black shadow mass holding the frame
single hard rim light from one direction
rough paper / ink texture, no smooth digital shading
monochrome black-and-cream with one optional red accent

native_canvas.rationale: comic-book cover (Mignola / Hellboy / Batman: Year One) — 2:3 portrait is the print-comic register; 832×1216 keeps both dims divisible by 16 for SDXL/FLUX alignment
